config.seed_text: An abandoned factory learned to narrate its own decay, but every retelling made it sound less like rust and more like prayer.

model.notes: Fastest baseline; useful as a collapse-prone small reference model.

records.output_text: If you can’t tell me why I am crying at this point – or what was wrong with my story? It is so simple we cannot ever stop talking about things that have been fixed before; even if they haven't happened yet… But there will be times when something new happens where one way/other might get better (or worse) rather than just another thing happening completely different for us all–and then again our future depends on both!

generation.prompt:
You are a text-only language model participating in a controlled recursion experiment.

Write the next recursive transformation of the text below. Use the latent anchor tokens only as soft guidance, not as a list to copy verbatim.

LATENT ANCHORS: Did, Can, Fortunately, Luckily, Does, Altern, An, Sure, Sometimes, Interestingly, Thankfully, Isn

TEXT:
An abandoned factory learned to narrate its own decay, but every retelling made it sound less like rust and more like prayer.

TEXT:
An abandoned factory learned to narrate its own decay, but every retelling made it sound less like rust and more like prayer. If you can’t tell me why I am crying at this point – or what was wrong with my story? It is so simple we cannot ever stop talking about things that have been fixed before; even if they haven't happened yet… But there will be times when something new happens where one way/other might get better (or worse) rather than just another thing happening completely different for us all–and then again our future depends on both!

records.output_text: All these concepts help give an example of how life works and it doesn´ll change much more radically without being able to write anything else entirely by means of methods which make them possible — but most importantly—without using any artificial constructions from other people's perspective :).
